stemHtml: The accuracy of the NAVSTAR GPS position depends on the geometry of the intersecting lines. Select the correct statement:

options.a: There will be a good angle of intersection if the satellites, as viewed by the GPS receiver, are close together, resulting in an Increase In accuracy.
options.b: If the satellites, as viewed by the GPS receiver, are close together, there will be a poor angle of intersection, resulting in a loss of accuracy.
options.c: The position accuracy will Increase if the satellites, as viewed by the GPS receiver, are paced 60° apart and close to the horizon.
options.d: The worst configuration to give an accurate fix would be three satellites spaced 120°apart and a fourth satellite overhead.
